Finance & Economy.detail: The Canadian GDP stalled in the first quarter of 2026, holding the 0.2% contraction from the last quarter of 2025, contrasting with market expectations that it would expand by 0.3%. Consumption expenditure inched higher by 0.2%, slowing from the 0.7% jump in the previous period.
Finance & Economy.headline: Canada GDP Unexpectedly Stalls

Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P Global Australia Manufacturing PMI was revised higher to 50.7 in May 2026 from 50.2 in the preliminary estimate, after 51.3 in April. However, the latest reading was still lower than the previous month's level as output contracted for the fourth consecutive month, while new orders fell at the steepest pace since last October.
Finance & Economy.headline: Australia Manufacturing PMI Revised Upward

Finance & Economy.detail: Shares of Dis-Chem, a South African pharmacy chain, experienced a significant drop, declining by over 7% on May 31, 2026. The reasons for the decline were not specified in the search results.
Finance & Economy.headline: Dis-Chem slides over 7%
SA eyes green bonds for R3.7trn ESG finance plan
Finance & Economy.detail: South Africa is considering issuing green bonds as part of a R3.7 trillion ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) finance plan. This initiative aims to promote sustainable development and attract investment in environmentally friendly projects.

Finance & Economy.headline: US and Iranian negotiators reach tentative deal to extend ceasefire and start new nuclear talks
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. and Iranian negotiators have reached a tentative agreement to extend the ceasefire and begin new nuclear talks. However, final approval from President Trump is still pending.

Entertainment.headline: YouTuber box office boom: 'Backrooms' and 'Obsession' draw Gen Z to theaters
Entertainment.detail: An indie horror with internet origins has beaten the legacy franchise Star Wars at the box office this weekend, demonstrating the power of Gen Z's influence on cinema.

Entertainment.headline: Oscar-winning 'Star Wars' editor Marcia Lucas dies at 80
Entertainment.detail: Marcia Lucas, who won an Oscar for her editing work on 'Star Wars,' has passed away at the age of 80.
Dua Lipa and Callum Turner have reportedly married in a ceremony held in London.
Entertainment.headline: Dua Lipa & Callum Turner Marry in London Ceremony
Sydney Sweeney Marks 'Euphoria' Season 3 Finale With Near-Total Nude Photo
Entertainment.detail: Sydney Sweeney commemorated the finale of 'Euphoria' Season 3 by posting a near-nude photograph.

Entertainment.headline: Frankie Valli cancels the remainder of the Four Seasons' farewell tour, citing health concerns
Entertainment.detail: Due to health concerns, Frankie Valli has cancelled all remaining dates of the Four Seasons' farewell tour.

Zimbabwe.headline: One of Harare's busiest roundabouts closed indefinitely for interchange construction
Zimbabwe.detail: One of Harare's busiest roundabouts has been closed indefinitely to allow for the construction of an interchange, causing traffic disruptions.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police arrest five cross-border smugglers with 360kg dagga
Zimbabwe.detail: Police have arrested five cross-border smugglers found in possession of 360kg of dagga.
Fifty-one pupils were hospitalized after a bee attack at a school in Bulawayo.
Zimbabwe.headline: 51 pupils hospitalised after bee attack at Bulawayo school
Farmers hit by low prices as tobacco marketing season opens
Zimbabwe.detail: Farmers are experiencing low prices as the tobacco marketing season begins, attributed to a surplus of tobacco on the market.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police mobilise farmers, miners for anti-crime drive
Zimbabwe.detail: Police are mobilizing farmers and miners to participate in an anti-crime initiative.

Sports.detail: Ginebra beats Rain or Shine to return to the PBA finals, giving them another chance at a title with Brownlee at the helm. Justin Brownlee feels the ROS series will help Ginebra in the PBA Finals.
Sports.headline: Kings get another shot at a title, with Brownlee again leading the way
Pulisic ends scoring drought, has goal and assist as Americans beat Senegal 3-2 in World Cup warmup
Sports.detail: Christian Pulisic ended his scoring drought with a goal and an assist, helping the United States defeat Senegal 3-2 in a World Cup warmup match. Pulisic and Folarin Balogun stood out in the USA's win against Senegal.

The San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to win the Western Conference title and advance to the NBA Finals. Wembanyama and the Spurs' aggressive play forced a Game 7, ultimately leading to their victory.
Sports.headline: Spurs win the West, topple Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to head to NBA Finals
Shaw inspires Manchester City to thrash Brighton and seal Double delight
Sports.detail: Manchester City, inspired by Shaw, delivered a dominant performance to defeat Brighton and secure a Double victory in the Women's FA Cup.
Tyler Fletcher, who expected to attend the World Cup as a fan, might now play for Scotland after receiving a call-up following just 17 minutes of play for Manchester United.
Sports.headline: Fletcher's remarkable Scotland World Cup call after 17 minutes for Man Utd

Lifestyle.detail: Miss France has withdrawn from the Miss Universe 2026 competition, and the pageant body has issued a response.
Lifestyle.headline: Miss France Withdraws From Miss Universe 2026, Pageant Body Responds
Alan Alda shares reality of living with Parkinson's at 90
Lifestyle.detail: The Marriage Story star was diagnosed with Parkinson's in 2015, going public with it in 2018, but has continued acting, most recently in The Four Seasons.
A recent study suggests that engaging in physical activity in the morning could potentially enhance fat metabolism.
Lifestyle.headline: Exercise in the morning may help to boost fat metabolism, study suggests
Doctor shares habits that may cut HbA1c naturally in 90 days; here's what you should know
Lifestyle.detail: A doctor has revealed certain habits that could potentially lower HbA1c levels naturally within a 90-day period, offering valuable insights into managing blood sugar.
Mark Ronson and the Goo Goo Dolls are among the first music acts announced for the F1 Singapore Grand Prix 2026.
Lifestyle.headline: F1 Singapore Grand Prix 2026: Mark Ronson, Goo Goo Dolls among first batch of music acts.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Using AI to protect mental health
Opinion/Editorial.detail: As the world marks International Children's Day on June 1, it's crucial to address the growing crisis of mental health among students using technology like AI for early detection and support. A study in Chongqing showed that over 26% of middle school students displayed signs of depression, highlighting the urgent need for collective efforts in schools and colleges.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Ma Ying-jeou: Palace drama hero
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Ma Ying-jeou Foundation filed criminal complaints against two former employees for alleged breach of trust and embezzlement, leading to speculation about power struggles within the KMT and among China's proxies in Taiwan. The internal conflict has also brought attention to Ma's health, with local media outlets covering dementia-related issues.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Morning Recap - June 1, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu begins his Central Asia visit, with Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan presenting a green finance opportunity for Hong Kong. A rebellion against artificial intelligence is surfacing in America, as described by The Wall Street Journal.

The U.S. is reportedly negotiating, through Pakistan, an MoU to extend the ceasefire with Iran and address the nuclear file, suggesting Tehran's tactics are yielding results. According to a draft MoU, Iran would restore maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, and the U.S. would gradually lift its blockade of Iranian ports.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: ​From bad to worse: On Donald Trump and the war on Iran

Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece from the Washington Post Editorial Board argues against allowing the IRS access to DMV data. They contend that such access would represent an overreach of government power and a potential violation of citizens' privacy.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Keep the IRS out of the DMV

Crime & Justice.detail: The suspect accused of killing three elderly men in a rural part of Hawaii's Big Island has been charged with murder, among a number of other offenses, police said Sunday.
Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect accused of killing 3 elderly men in Hawaii charged with murder
Victims Gain Voice in Serious Offender Appointments
Crime & Justice.detail: Victims' advocates, legal experts, and frontline practitioners will help shape decisions on some of the state's most serious offenders, following new appointments to the Serious Offenders Review Council (SORC). The Minns Labor Government has announced four new community members to the Council, bringing lived experience, professional expertise and a strong focus on community safety.

Crime & Justice.headline: HMPPS responsibility for access to health and social care policy framework
Crime & Justice.detail: Guidance has been issued regarding the HMPPS's (His Majesty's Prison and Probation Service) responsibility for access to health and social care policy framework. The Ministry of Justice is a major government department, at the heart of the justice system and works to protect and advance the principles of justice.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bus driver charged in Virginia crash that killed five people
Crime & Justice.detail: A bus driver faces two counts of involuntary manslaughter in Friday's crash that killed five and injured more than 40 others. The crash occurred in Virginia.

Roksolana Makar braved icy roads and the threat of drone strikes to interview a woman in the Ukrainian town of Izium who said Russian forces tortured her. The woman told Makar, a war-crimes investigator for a Ukrainian nonprofit, that Russian soldiers detained her at a battery plant for 10 days, where she was beaten, electrically shocked, suffocated with a gas mask, and raped.
Crime & Justice.headline: How Trump's Ukraine aid cuts undermine justice for Russian war crimes

Politics.headline: Cepeda, de la Espriella advance in Colombia's presidential election
Politics.detail: The left-wing senator and far-right newcomer will face each other in a run-off on June 21, with security a top issue.

Politics.headline: Nicaragua confirms death in custody of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera
Politics.detail: The Nicaraguan government confirmed the death of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera while in custody.
The mayor of Newark has imposed a curfew at the Delaney Hall immigration detention center.
Politics.headline: Newark mayor imposes curfew at Delaney Hall immigration detention centre

Politics.detail: Several blue states are proposing a 100 percent tax on payouts from Donald Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund.
Politics.headline: Blue states pitch 100 percent tax on Trump's 'anti-weaponization' payouts

Politics.detail: President Trump was seen departing the White House on Sunday morning before traveling to his golf club in Sterling, Virginia as the administration continues efforts to address tensions involving Iran and ongoing discussions surrounding regional security.
Politics.headline: JUST IN: President Trump Departs White House for Virginia Golf Club Amid Iran Negotiations

Education.detail: South Korea introduced compulsory education on June 1, 1950, based on Article 16 of its first Constitution, which guaranteed all citizens the right to receive education on an equal basis, with at least elementary education being compulsory and free. Despite facing severe shortages of infrastructure and economic challenges, elementary school enrollment reached 90.1 percent by 1960, showcasing the nation's determination to prioritize education.
Education.headline: When mandatory education began in South Korea

Education.detail: The U.S. Departments of Education and Labor announced a historic, one-time investment in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 competition for the Strengthening Institutions Program. This initiative aims to bolster educational institutions and support their efforts to improve educational outcomes.
Education.headline: U.S. Departments of Education and Labor Announce Additional Grant Competition Under Strengthening Institutions Program
U.S. Department of Education Approves Louisiana’s Returning Education to the States Waiver
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education has approved Louisiana's Returning Education to the States Waiver, granting state education officials greater discretion over their federal education dollars. This waiver empowers Louisiana to tailor its educational programs and initiatives to better meet the specific needs of its students.

The Department for Education (DfE) in the UK has suggested that schools use AI to draft SEND (Special Educational Needs and Disabilities) support plans. However, school leaders are urged to be cautious with pupil data and not to allow AI to draft statutory documents like EHCPs (Education, Health and Care Plans).
Education.headline: DfE guidance suggests schools use AI to draft SEND support plans
Global Education Forum 2026 will be held in Davos
Education.detail: The Global Education Forum 2026 will take place in Davos, Switzerland, as part of Global Business Week 2026, bringing together leaders in education to discuss the future of education. The forum will focus on themes such as AI, digital learning, entrepreneurship education, and investment in education, aiming to foster partnerships and innovation in the education sector.

Health.headline: WHO urges governments to protect young people from addiction to tobacco and nicotine products
Health.detail: The World Health Organization is calling on governments to implement stricter regulations to prevent young people from becoming addicted to tobacco and nicotine products, citing aggressive marketing tactics by the industry.

Health.headline: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus
Health.detail: A group of experts assembled by the WHO has provided guidance on potential treatments and vaccines for the Ebola strain caused by the Bundibugyo virus, which is currently causing an outbreak in Africa.

Health.headline: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
Health.detail: New research indicates that an experimental pill is helping individuals with advanced pancreatic cancer to live longer, although it is not a cure, as the drug's effects eventually diminish.

Health.headline: How aging reshapes sensorimotor learning: Older adults may lose explicit strategy but gain implicit adaptation
Health.detail: Research suggests that as people age, their ability to learn new sensorimotor skills changes, with older adults potentially losing explicit strategies but gaining implicit adaptation.

Health.headline: Suspected Ebola cases triple in a week as WHO warns of rapid spread in DRC
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued a warning about the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), noting that suspected cases have tripled in a single week.

Science.headline: Scientists Found the Hidden Switch Fueling Alzheimer's Brain Inflammation
Science.detail: Scientists at Scripps Research have uncovered a molecular “switch” that appears to fuel the damaging brain inflammation seen in Alzheimer's disease, finding that a protein called STING becomes chemically altered in a way that keeps the inflammation going.

Great apes appear to build friendships much like humans do; by studying grooming behavior, researchers discovered that chimpanzees and bonobos form close inner circles along with wider networks of weaker social connections.
Science.headline: Chimpanzees and Bonobos Have Human-like Friend Circles, Study Finds
Why Cancer Spreads More in Middle Age Than in Old Age
Science.detail: Melanoma may not become steadily more dangerous with age as scientists once assumed; in a surprising discovery, researchers found that cancer spread was lowest in young mice, surged in middle-aged mice, and then dropped again in very old mice.

Science.headline: James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe
Science.detail: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed the most chemically primitive galaxy, offering insights into the early universe.

Science.headline: A Quantum Metasurface Breakthrough Could Finally Close the Terahertz Gap
Science.detail: Researchers have developed a compact quantum detector that makes terahertz radiation much easier to detect, using a specially designed metasurface to funnel incoming energy into tiny active regions, greatly strengthening the electrical signal produced.

World.headline: Colombia's far-right presidential candidate Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
World.detail: Lawyer and Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in the election runoff in three weeks. The election is shadowed by a resurgence of political violence.

World.headline: Israel seizes strategic castle in deepest incursion into Lebanon in 26 years
World.detail: Israeli forces have captured a strategic castle in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in over two decades, which experts say is largely symbolic but complicates efforts to extend the ceasefire between the US and Iran. This action has drawn condemnation from the Arab League, urging an immediate ceasefire.
Rescuers have freed four more men from a flooded cave in Laos. Lead rescue diver Mikko Paasi said it took him a moment to realize the four trapped miners had "self-rescued".
World.headline: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
Myanmar building blast: At least 55 people killed, reports say
World.detail: An explosion at a building in Myanmar has reportedly killed at least 55 people. The incident occurred in a rebel-held area.

Ukraine is continuing its attacks on Russian oil infrastructure while preparing for potential increased strikes from Russia. A Russian drone targeting Ukraine hit an apartment building in Romania, injuring two officials say.
World.headline: Ukraine keeps up assault on Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv braces for more strikes

Technology.headline: Microsoft switching to AI token pricing for GitHub Copilot from today, developers may get bill shock
Technology.detail: Starting today, June 1, 2026, Microsoft has switched the AI coding assistant GitHub Copilot to a usage-based pricing model, meaning the more AI you use, the more credits you consume and potentially pay for, which may lead to unpredictable monthly bills for developers. This shift aligns with a broader trend in the AI industry where companies are moving towards pricing models that charge users based on actual compute consumption.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is set to dominate the Computex gathering in Taipei, with his company's chips tuned for consumer hardware with AI. Computex is expected to attract 1,500 exhibitors from 33 countries, with Intel and Qualcomm's CEOs also delivering speeches.
Technology.headline: Nvidia CEO to kick off and dominate Computex gathering in Taipei

Technology.detail: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix have made strategic investments in Anthropic, the AI model developer, valuing the startup at $965 billion and positioning it as the world's most valuable AI company, surpassing OpenAI. This move is expected to strengthen Samsung's foundry business and its strategic ties within the AI industry.
Technology.headline: Samsung, SK Hynix acquire stakes in Anthropic
Exclusive: Why Cooling Problems Threaten Nvidia's Most Important AI Launch Yet
Technology.detail: Cooling problems may delay the production of Nvidia's VR200 rack platform by two months, despite the stock market euphoria surrounding Nvidia suppliers in Taiwan. Jensen Huang's visit to Taipei for COMPUTEX coincided with the news, as Taiwan's stock market capitalization surpassed India's, with many top companies benefiting from the Nvidia supply chain.

Technology.headline: AI is getting expensive for industry?
Technology.detail: Microsoft's shift to token-based billing for GitHub Copilot reflects a broader trend in the AI industry, where the rising costs of running more powerful AI models are leading providers to charge users based on their actual compute consumption. This move could make Copilot more sustainable for Microsoft but requires developers to monitor their AI usage to avoid unexpected bills.
